headline: NVIDIA Announces Open Physical AI Data Factory Blueprint to Accelerate Robotics, Vision AI Agents and Autonomous Vehicle Development

headline: Uber Stock Rises After Nvidia Partner Update
summary: Uber stock was rising Monday night after Nvidia announced an expanded partnership with the ride-hailing platform.  Nvidia and Uber announced an expanded partnership to launch a fleet of autonomous vehicles powered by the full-stack NVIDIA DRIVE AV software across 28 cities and four continents by 2028.  Lyft stock was rising 3% after announcing it will use Nvidia AI to enhance the company’s machine learning systems across global operations.

raw.headline: Uber Stock Rises After Nvidia Partner Update

Nvidia and Uber announced an expanded partnership to launch a fleet of autonomous vehicles powered by the full-stack NVIDIA DRIVE AV software across 28 cities and four continents by 2028.  Lyft stock was rising 3% after announcing it will use Nvidia AI to enhance the company’s machine learning systems across global operations.

headline: Uber, Nvidia plan robotaxi rollout in 28 cities starting next year
summary: Uber Technologies and Nvidia said on Monday they will deploy a fleet of robotaxis powered by Nvidia's autonomous driving software ‌on the ride-hailing network, starting in Los Angeles and San Francisco ‌in 2027 and expanding to 28 cities globally by 2028.  Alphabet's Waymo currently operates the ‌most advanced commercial robotaxi service, ⁠running fully driverless rides in cities including Phoenix, San Francisco and Los Angeles, and steadily expanding its fleet.  Uber and Nvidia said the ​vehicles ​will run on the DRIVE Hyperion autonomous ​vehicle platform along with Alpamayo, a ‌reasoning-based AI model designed to handle complex road scenarios.

headline: Uber to roll out Nvidia-powered, self-driving taxis in 2027
summary: Uber will begin offering Nvidia-powered self-driving taxis in Los Angeles and San Francisco in 2027.

headline: NVIDIA to Launch L4 Software-Driven Robotaxis on Uber Across 28 Cities by 2028
summary: SAN FRANCISCO & SANTA CLARA, Calif., March 16, 2026--Uber Technologies, Inc. (NYSE: UBER) and NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) today announced an expansion of their autonomous vehicle partnership. The companies, supported by their growing roster of automaker partners, plan to launch a global fleet of entirely NVIDIA software-driven autonomous vehicles, starting in Los Angeles and San Francisco in the first half of 2027 and scaling across 28 cities globally by 2028.

raw.headline: NVIDIA to Launch L4 Software-Driven Robotaxis on Uber Across 28 Cities by 2028

The companies, supported by their growing roster of automaker partners, plan to launch a global fleet of entirely NVIDIA software-driven autonomous vehicles, starting in Los Angeles and San Francisco in the first half of 2027 and scaling across 28 cities globally by 2028.

headline: BYD, Geely, Isuzu and Nissan Adopt NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion for Level 4 Vehicles
summary: Leading Automakers and Mobility Providers Build on NVIDIA’s End-to-End Autonomous Vehicle Platform as NVIDIA Advances Level 4 Hardware NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion Level 4 NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion provides a production-ready reference architecture for autonomous vehicles, integrating accelerated compute, sensors, networking and safety systems to enable scalable level 4 autonomy, with global automakers including BYD, Geely, Isuzu and Nissan building next-generation vehicles on the platform. News Summary: B

headline: Another billionaire confirms California exit: Uber co-founder Kalanick
summary: Kalanick, who launched a new robotics startup Atoms, told tech talk show TBPN that he became a Texas resident in December.

headline: Billionaire Uber co-founder Travis Kalanick admits strategically moving to Texas before California wealth tax
summary: Uber co-founder Travis Kalanick joins California's billionaire exodus, moving to Austin before a proposed wealth tax could cost him a one-time fee of $180 million.

headline: Uber Expands Into Parking And Autonomy As Valuation Gap Persists
summary: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) has agreed to acquire parking platform SpotHero to offer in-app parking experiences. The company is also formally launching Uber Autonomous Solutions to support global autonomous vehicle partner deployments. These moves expand Uber's platform beyond rides and delivery into commuter parking and dedicated services for autonomous partners. Uber Technologies, trading at $73.33, is adding a new layer to its platform by bringing parking and autonomous services under...

headline: Lucid’s Midsize EV And Robotaxi Push Confronts Ongoing Share Price Pressure
summary: Lucid Group (NasdaqGS:LCID) has introduced an all new midsize EV platform featuring the Cosmos and Earth SUVs, targeting a broader slice of the electric vehicle market. The company also outlined expanded robotaxi plans, including concept vehicles and fleet partnerships with Uber and Nuro. Lucid highlighted in house AI systems and manufacturing refinements aimed at improving efficiency and scalability across its operations.
